changed.from: Design paired localized fuzzy-hypothesis mixtures that perturb mu_P on treatment scale h_mu and pi_P on treatment scale h_pi, renormalize each pi_P(.|x) to integrate to one, keep the marginal treatment law fixed near t_0, and compare a pure-mu channel against a coupled (mu_P, pi_P) channel to test whether any genuine beta-dominated elbow survives.
changed.to: Lower-side resolution and remaining frontier handle. Define the beta-free lower rate underline_rho_n(alpha,s,d)=rho_n=n^{-2*alpha/(2*alpha+1)} vee n^{-2/(1+d/(4*s)+1/alpha)}. For comparison with the published HOIF upper analysis, define delta=alpha wedge beta and overline_rho_n(alpha,beta,s,d)=n^{-2*delta/(2*delta+1)} vee n^{-2/(1+d/(4*s)+1/delta)}. The proved construction is the subclass/embedding argument: for beta<alpha, the smoother treatment-density class with beta'=alpha and a reduced Holder radius embeds into the beta-class, so class monotonicity transfers the beta>=alpha lower bound and gives R_n >= c underline_rho_n for every beta>0. The unresolved sharp-frontier handle is now explicit: either construct a single estimator over the frozen unrestricted class with risk O(underline_rho_n) when beta<alpha, proving no beta-dominated phase, or construct a normalized paired (mu,pi) fuzzy-hypothesis lower bound with separation asymptotically larger than underline_rho_n and no larger than the published upper envelope overline_rho_n.
changed.reason: The original handle named the desired perturbation style but did not expose a closed mathematical object. The proof shows that the lower frontier always contains the same rho_n floor by embedding a smoother beta'=alpha subclass into the beta<alpha class. What remains is not the existence of the rho_n lower bound but the exact beta<alpha sharpness question: closing the gap requires either a beta-insensitive upper estimator or a genuine beta-channel converse.

changed.to: There exist a density p_0 on [0,1]^d, a conditional treatment density q_0 on [0,1], and a constant eta_0>0 such that p_0 and q_0 have Holder norms at most M-eta_0 in the smoothness classes required of p_{X,P} and pi_P, q_0(a) >= c_0+eta_0 for all a in [t_0-epsilon_0,t_0+epsilon_0], p_0 is bounded above by M-eta_0, and the outcome mean zero has outcome-support slack inside [-M,M].
changed.reason: The lower-bound proof needs an interior baseline law around which small positive and negative perturbations can be made while preserving positivity, density normalization, bounded outcomes, and the declared Holder radii. Without such slack the class may be empty or may have no room for a two-sided local testing submodel.

directive:
D0 DIRECTIVE: Re-solve forward from the D0.5 failure by replacing the singular deterministic-outcome lower-bound families with the standard dominated noisy-outcome experiment. This is a new direction for the construction, not a rewind and not a proof-only edit: edit the perturbation family definition and any affected class bookkeeping so the lower-bound submodels have Y | (A=a,X=x) = mu_xi(a,x) + epsilon with epsilon ~ N(0,sigma^2), fixed sigma^2=1 acceptable, independent of (A,X) and of the signs. Realize the causal version by taking Y(a)=mu_xi(a,X)+epsilon with the same independent mean-zero noise, so theta_P(t_0)=E_P[mu_P(t_0,X)] remains a function of the observed law P and is not encoded in a side assumption. If the current core still has a bounded deterministic outcome channel, replace that lower-bound outcome-channel node with the fixed-variance/sub-Gaussian dominated channel needed for the standard minimax experiment; do not return to deterministic Y.

ORACLE BRANCH: Repair lem:interior-treatment-regression-lower using Tsybakov 2009, Chapter 2, two-point testing for Gaussian regression. Keep X and A|X fixed at the slack baseline and set mu_+(a,x)-mu_-(a,x)=2 lambda h_n^alpha psi((a-t_0)/h_n), with psi compactly supported in the interior treatment window and lambda fixed small. Under the common N(0,sigma^2) outcome noise, the one-observation KL is exactly (1/(2 sigma^2)) E[(mu_+(A,X)-mu_-(A,X))^2] = O(lambda^2 h_n^{2 alpha+1}), finite because the two laws have overlapping Gaussian conditional support. Choose h_n asymp n^{-1/(2 alpha+1)} so n KL=O(1), while |theta_{P_+}(t_0)-theta_{P_-}(t_0)| asymp h_n^alpha; Le Cam/Tsybakov then gives MSE risk at least c n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)}. This branch is valid for every beta>0 because pi is unchanged.

FUZZY/FUNCTIONAL BRANCH: Rebuild lem:localized-fuzzy-hoif-lower only if the standard localized fuzzy calculation can be made explicit under the noisy channel. Use the Robins-Li-Tchetgen-Tchetgen-van der Vaart 2008 HOIF lower-bound machinery and the Kennedy-Balakrishnan-Robins-Wasserman AoS 2024 localized fuzzy hypotheses/CATE converse template, transported to the continuous-treatment localization. Work in the s<d/4 deficient regime with r_n=n^{-1/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha)}, h_n=r_n^{1/alpha}, ell_n=r_n^{1/(2s)}, and K_n asymp ell_n^{-d} disjoint covariate cubes. On each cube put an independent sign xi_j and an order-s Holder bump phi_j with amplitude of order ell_n^s, normalized explicitly so ||phi_j||_{C^s} is uniformly bounded and the integrated block expression used in theta_P(t_0) has the claimed constant-order normalization; do not merely assert that sum_j xi_j phi_j(x)/sqrt(K_n) has the right size. Tensor the covariate bumps with an order-alpha treatment bump psi((a-t_0)/h_n) for mu_xi, and if a treatment-density perturbation is used for the HOIF/product structure, use a mean-zero treatment bump kappa((a-t_0)/h_n) so pi_xi integrates to one and remains positive. Derive the exact theta-separation: it must be |theta under prior + minus theta under prior -| asymp r_n, hence squared-risk rate r_n^2=n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha)}. Derive the finite noisy-channel chi^2 affinity rather than reusing the broken deterministic argument: condition on xi,xi', compute the Gaussian likelihood contribution through exp{sigma^{-2} E[mu_xi(A,X) mu_xi'(A,X)]} together with any treatment-density likelihood factors, reduce the mixture affinity to the Ingster/Robins product over cubes, and show E_xi E_xi'[prod_j(1+per-cube contiguity term)] is bounded. The displayed scaling must be derived from the chosen normalization, including the intended n h_n r_n/sqrt(K_n) asymp 1 or its corrected equivalent; if this scaling or the theta-separation changes, update the conclusion honestly.

DOWNSTREAM REPAIR: Only after the two support lemmas are repaired may thm:sharp-pointwise-lower-bound, lem:rho-lower-bound-all-beta, and oeq:full-beta-frontier reuse them. Do not add an x-smoothness assumption on pi to force the fuzzy branch. Do not strengthen ass:beta-dominates-alpha. Do not move the estimand away from theta_P(t_0)=E_P[mu_P(t_0,X)]. The noisy outcome channel is the standard construction that makes the submodels mutually contiguous; it is not a laundering assumption and it does not change the target rates.

HONEST FALLBACK REQUIRED: If, after introducing the fixed noisy outcome channel, the localized-fuzzy r_n^2 branch still cannot be rigorously discharged with both finite chi^2 affinity and the claimed theta-separation under the frozen assumptions, stop trying to patch it. Demote the s<d/4 functional-rate branch n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha)} to an explicit open-ended question, and make the proved headline lower bound the oracle theorem R_n >= c n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)} for all alpha,s,d and beta>0. Then keep prop:oracle-regime-reduction as the matching statement: when s>=d/4 this oracle lower bound equals rho_n and matches the published Bonvini-Kennedy HOIF upper rate in that regime. This fallback is an honest target adjustment, not a weakening-to-prove: it delivers the first valid minimax lower bound for the interior dose-response curve and leaves the unresolved fuzzy functional branch visible as an OEQ.

PART A -- RIGORIZE THE SAME-CLASS UPPER THEOREM. Make lem:same-class-hoif-upper-smooth-covariates a self-contained in-core theorem, not a proof sketch. Prove it under exactly the original class P_{alpha,beta,s}, local positivity, boundedness, and the stated Holder assumptions; do not add a crux-encoding assumption and do not use an oracle nuisance. The proof must proceed as follows. (i) Define K_h-localized cross-fitting folds and training-fold nuisance estimators for mu, pi or g=1/pi, and p_X using local-polynomial smoothing in the treatment coordinate and a J-dimensional covariate sieve/basis. State and prove the root-L2 bounds after boundedness/positivity truncation: ||muhat-mu||_{K_h,2} <= C(h^alpha+J^{-s/d}+sqrt(J/(n h))) and the analogous bound for g and p_X, with beta-dependent treatment-density estimation terms isolated inside the high-order nuisance remainder and shown harmless for each fixed beta by the chosen HOIF order. Derive these rates from the Holder approximation and empirical least-squares/concentration lemmas; do not cite them as asserted facts. (ii) Define the localized projection-HOIF estimator explicitly: target theta_0(t0), smoothed/local-polynomial target theta_h(t0), bandwidth h_n asymp n^{-1/(2 alpha+1)}, covariate projection dimension J_n asymp n h_n, and HOIF order m_n increasing slowly enough to dominate the nuisance-product remainder while preserving the degenerate U-statistic variance bounds. (iii) Give the full MSE decomposition, with derivations of every displayed term: E[(hattheta-theta_0(t0))^2] <= C{h_n^{2 alpha}+(n h_n)^{-1}+J_n^{-4s/d}+J_n/(n h_n)^2}+Rem_n. Derive h_n^{2 alpha} from the local-polynomial smoothing bias; derive (n h_n)^{-1} from the first-order localized influence-function variance; derive J_n^{-4s/d} from the product of the two covariate projection residuals; derive J_n/(n h_n)^2 from the second/higher-order degenerate projected U-statistic kernel; and give a separate lemma bounding Rem_n=o(n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)}) by the cross-fit nuisance rates, truncation, and the selected m_n. (iv) Perform the final algebra explicitly: with h_n asymp n^{-1/(2 alpha+1)} and J_n asymp n h_n, h_n^{2 alpha}=(n h_n)^{-1}=J_n/(n h_n)^2=asymp n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)}, while J_n^{-4s/d} <= n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)} exactly when s>=d/4. Conclude sup_{P in P_{alpha,beta,s}} E_P[(hattheta-theta_0(t0))^2] <= C n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)} for every fixed beta in the smooth-covariate regime s>=d/4. Then update thm:sharp-minimax-smooth-covariate so it is genuinely two-sided: combine thm:sharp-pointwise-lower-bound with this rigorous same-class upper theorem.

PART B -- REFRAME THE HEADLINE AND OPEN QUESTION. Edit def:beta-frontier-handle, the abstract/introduction framing, and oeq:full-beta-frontier so the delivered contribution is not presented as the full unrestricted beta frontier. The headline contribution must be: first, an all-beta minimax lower bound R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s},t0) >= c n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)} obtained by the de-laundered Bernoulli two-point/Le Cam construction perturbing only mu with P_X and A|X fixed at the slack baseline; second, the sharp same-class minimax characterization R_n asymp n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)} in the smooth-covariate regime s>=d/4, using the in-core HOIF upper theorem from Part A. The unrestricted s<d/4 and beta<alpha sharp frontier must be stated as an explicit secondary open question/future direction: it is unknown whether the sharp rate remains at the oracle treatment-regression floor, rises to an alpha-only functional branch, or becomes genuinely beta-sensitive. Do not try to close this by perturbing the covariate structure or by changing the model class to make a beta-sensitive lower branch appear; that would launder the claim. Keep oeq:full-beta-frontier as the honest unresolved frontier and make clear it is not the headline result.

Add lem:same-class-hoif-upper-deficient-covariates (kind: lemma). Statement: for every fixed beta > 0 and every 0 < s < d/4, the same cross-fit projection-HOIF estimator on P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0), with the covariate sieve dimension re-optimized for the deficient regime, satisfies sup_P E_P[(hattheta - theta_P(t_0))^2] <= C n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha)} for all large n. Dependencies: lem:localized-projection-hoif-risk-decomposition, lem:bounded-sieve-local-polynomial-rates, and the class assumptions only. Proof directive: start from E[(hattheta-theta)^2] <= C{h_n^{2 alpha} + (n h_n)^{-1} + J_n^{-4s/d} + J_n/(n h_n)^2} + Rem_n. Balance J_n^{-4s/d} with J_n/(n h_n)^2, giving J_n ~ (n h_n)^{2d/(d+4s)} and hence both covariate terms ~ (n h_n)^{-8s/(d+4s)}. Since s < d/4, 8s/(d+4s) < 1, so this functional covariate term dominates the oracle variance term (n h_n)^{-1}. Optimize h_n^{2 alpha} against (n h_n)^{-8s/(d+4s)}. With D_s = 4s + alpha(d+4s), take h_n ~ n^{-4s/D_s} and J_n ~ n^{2 alpha d/D_s}. The resulting exponent is 8 alpha s/D_s = 2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha). Use the same nuisance/sieve/local-polynomial choices and the same m_n argument as in the smooth-covariate upper proof to keep Rem_n = o(n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha)}). Round J_n to an integer without changing rates.

Add thm:frontier-bracket-deficient (kind: theorem). Statement: for every fixed beta > 0 and every 0 < s < d/4, for all large n, c n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)} <= R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0), t_0) <= C n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha)}. Dependencies: def:minimax-risk, lem:oracle-dose-regression-lower-all-beta, lem:same-class-hoif-upper-deficient-covariates. Proof directive: the lower endpoint is exactly the oracle treatment-regression floor from lem:oracle-dose-regression-lower-all-beta, valid for all s because the perturbation varies only mu while holding P_X and A|X fixed. The upper endpoint is the deficient-regime HOIF functional branch from lem:same-class-hoif-upper-deficient-covariates. State explicitly that the bounds are an honest loose two-sided bracket and do not match in this regime; when s < d/4, 2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha) < 2 alpha/(2 alpha+1).

Narrow oeq:full-beta-frontier. Replace the bare deficient-regime OEQ with a terminal OEQ depending on thm:frontier-bracket-deficient: determine the sharp deficient-regime exponent within the proved bracket c n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)} <= R_n <= C n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha)} for 0 < s < d/4 and fixed beta > 0. The open question is whether the minimax rate equals the oracle floor, equals the HOIF functional branch, or is an intermediate beta-sensitive exponent. Do not assert a matching converse for the HOIF branch.

Close the upper-side rigor objections in one pass as follows.

(A) RIGORIZE lem:bounded-sieve-local-polynomial-rates. Replace every occurrence of prose like standard theory gives with recorded cited inputs and then a short specialization argument. Add citation-leaf subresults, depending only on the class assumptions and the cited source:

1. Local-polynomial-in-A input. State: for scalar A, an interior point t, kernel K of order at least floor(eta), bandwidth h with n h -> infinity, density of A|X locally bounded above and away from zero, and eta-Holder smoothness in A, the local polynomial estimator has pointwise/L2 bias O(h^eta) and stochastic size O((n h)^(-1/2)) in the scalar problem; for J series coefficients estimated inside the A-window, the stochastic coefficient contribution is O_P(sqrt(J/(n h))). Cite Fan and Gijbels (1996, Local Polynomial Modelling and Its Applications, Ch. 3, Sections 3.1-3.3, bias/variance/asymptotic theory for local polynomial regression) and/or Tsybakov (2009, Introduction to Nonparametric Estimation, Ch. 1 local polynomial/kernel estimator results for Holder classes). Record the specialization used here as: for f(a,x) with eta-Holder smoothness in a and s-Holder smoothness in x, the cross-fit local-polynomial plus J-term x-sieve estimator satisfies ||hat f(t,.)-f(t,.)||_{L2(P_X)} <= C{h^eta + J^{-s/d} + sqrt(J/(n h))} with high probability/in L2, under the existing bounded Gram and moment assumptions.

2. Series/sieve L2 input. State: for an s-Holder function on a compact d-dimensional covariate support and a stable local basis/spline/wavelet sieve of dimension J, least-squares series estimation has ||hat f-f||_{L2} <= C{J^{-s/d}+sqrt(J/N)} for effective sample size N, assuming bounded moments and Gram eigenvalues bounded away from zero. Cite Newey (1997, Journal of Econometrics 79, Convergence rates and asymptotic normality for series estimators, Sections 2-3), Chen (2007, Handbook of Econometrics, Ch. 76, Large Sample Sieve Estimation of Semi-Nonparametric Models, sieve approximation and convergence-rate sections), and especially Belloni-Chernozhukov-Chetverikov-Kato (2015, Journal of Econometrics 186:345-366, Theorem 4.1 L2 rate of convergence and Section 3 approximation examples; arXiv:1212.0442). Use N=n h for the A-localized nuisance regressions and N=n for p_X if p_X is estimated globally.

3. Density/denominator input. State explicitly that bounded truncation and local positivity are deterministic controls: if g, pi, or p_X are bounded below by c>0 on the localized class and estimates are truncated to [c/2,2C], reciprocal maps are Lipschitz, e.g. ||1/hat g-1/g||_2 <= C||hat g-g||_2, so denominator errors inherit the same L2 rates. This is not a statistical theorem; it follows from the class positivity and truncation definition.

4. Beta role. Make clear that the treatment-density Holder beta enters only through the pi/g nuisance rate h^beta + J^{-s/d}+sqrt(J/(n h)) and through the high-order orthogonal remainder. No uniform-in-beta claim is needed: for each fixed beta>0 the rate exponent is positive, and the order m threshold in (B) can be chosen finite.

(B) ADD lem:hoif-order-remainder-negligible as an explicit DAG node. Statement: Let delta_mu,n, delta_pi,n, delta_x,n be the cross-fit L2 nuisance rates supplied by lem:bounded-sieve-local-polynomial-rates after the h_n,J_n choices of the relevant upper lemma, and let delta_n=max(delta_mu,n,delta_pi,n,delta_x,n). For the order-m_n projection-HOIF estimator, after separating the projection/truncation bias already accounted for in lem:localized-projection-hoif-risk-decomposition, the remaining orthogonal estimation remainder is bounded by a finite sum of products of m_n+1 cross-fit nuisance L2 errors; hence the MSE contribution satisfies Rem_n <= C delta_n^{2(m_n+1)} (or equivalently the absolute bias remainder is O(delta_n^{m_n+1})). Cite Robins, Li, Tchetgen Tchetgen and van der Vaart (2008, IMS Collections, Higher order influence functions and minimax estimation of nonlinear functionals, Section 2 higher-order IF/U-statistic theory and Section 3 statistical properties, especially the variance order and estimation-bias product display; DOI 10.1214/193940307000000527; arXiv:0805.3040) and the 2016 technical report arXiv:1601.05820 for the full proofs omitted from the IMS paper. Define the explicit threshold in the lemma: if the target MSE rate in the invoked upper lemma is rho_n=n^{-kappa+o(1)} and delta_n=O(n^{-lambda_*+o(1)}) with lambda_*=min{alpha eta_h, beta eta_h, s eta_J/d, (1-eta_h-eta_J)/2, (1-eta_J)/2}>0 for h_n=n^{-eta_h+o(1)}, J_n=n^{eta_J+o(1)}, then choose any integer m_n >= ceil(kappa/(2 lambda_*)). This gives Rem_n=o(rho_n). Instantiate kappa, eta_h, eta_J, and lambda_* separately inside the smooth-covariate and deficient-covariate upper lemmas, so the choose m large enough step is discharged by a named lemma, not prose.

(C) FIX beta_scope_mismatch in thm:frontier-bracket-deficient. Align the theorem quantifiers with the cited lemmas: state the deficient bracket as for every fixed beta>0 and every 0<s<d/4, under the declared same-class assumptions. The lower bound may remain all-beta if already proved that way, but the combined bracket must not advertise uniformity beyond the upper lemma. Ensure lem:same-class-hoif-upper-deficient-covariates has the same fixed-beta scope and that the theorem cites it with exactly that scope.

(D) REWIRE thm:sharp-minimax-smooth-covariate. Its upper half must be reproduced from DAG nodes only: class assumptions -> cited lem:bounded-sieve-local-polynomial-rates -> lem:localized-projection-hoif-risk-decomposition -> lem:hoif-order-remainder-negligible -> optimized h,J,m risk bound. Delete residual standard theory prose and delete any prose-only choose m large enough sentence. The theorem then inherits a fully cited upper proof; the lower half remains as before. Keep the graph acyclic: the nonparametric rate citation leaves depend only on definitions/class assumptions/citations, lem:hoif-order-remainder-negligible depends on the nuisance-rate lemma plus the HOIF citation, the two upper lemmas depend on rates + decomposition + remainder, and the two theorems depend on their upper/lower lemmas.

STRUCTURAL WIRING FIX (math content unchanged; edit ONLY the dependency graph). Break the 2-cycle lem:bounded-sieve-local-polynomial-rates <-> lem:hoif-order-remainder-negligible. The dependency must flow strictly forward: cited-source leaves -> nuisance-rate lemma -> remainder/risk-decomposition lemmas -> upper lemmas -> theorems -> oeq. Set depends_on EXACTLY:
- lem:bounded-sieve-local-polynomial-rates depends_on EXACTLY [lem:local-polynomial-window-source, lem:series-sieve-l2-source, lem:truncation-reciprocal-lipschitz] (plus the class assumptions/definitions it uses). It is a nuisance-RATE node established from its cited leaves only; it must NOT depend on lem:hoif-order-remainder-negligible, lem:localized-projection-hoif-risk-decomposition, any upper lemma, any theorem, or oeq.
- lem:hoif-order-remainder-negligible depends_on EXACTLY [lem:bounded-sieve-local-polynomial-rates, lem:hoif-remainder-product-source]. One-directional: the remainder USES the nuisance rates; the rates do NOT use the remainder.
- lem:localized-projection-hoif-risk-decomposition depends_on EXACTLY [lem:bounded-sieve-local-polynomial-rates, lem:hoif-projection-risk-source, lem:hoif-order-remainder-negligible].
- lem:same-class-hoif-upper-smooth-covariates and lem:same-class-hoif-upper-deficient-covariates each depends_on EXACTLY [lem:bounded-sieve-local-polynomial-rates, lem:localized-projection-hoif-risk-decomposition, lem:hoif-order-remainder-negligible].
- The cited-source leaves (lem:local-polynomial-window-source, lem:series-sieve-l2-source, lem:hoif-projection-risk-source, lem:hoif-remainder-product-source, lem:truncation-reciprocal-lipschitz) are LEAVES: depends_on only class assumptions/definitions and their external citation; they depend on NO other added lemma.
NO node may transitively depend on a node that depends on it. oeq:full-beta-frontier is the terminal consumer; nothing depends on it. Do not delete any cited-source leaf and do not reintroduce "standard theory gives" prose.
